_comment: Structural AI-writing rules for webdoc's native prose linter (scripts/lint_prose.py). No external dependency (reimplements the slice of Vale we need). 'kind': existence = regex search per prose line; occurrence = count regex matches per paragraph (lines joined with spaces), flag when >= min; staccato = run of short sentences in a paragraph. 'severity': error blocks the build (create_site.py), warning/suggestion only report. %s in a message is filled with the matched text (existence) or the count (occurrence/staccato). Regex is Python re syntax (JSON-escaped: \b, \s), applied to prose only (fenced code and inline code are stripped first).

rules.name: negative-parallelism
rules.message: Negative parallelism near '%s' - a single contrast is fine; if they cluster, state it directly instead of leaning on the contrast.

rules.name: stacked-negation
rules.message: Stacked negation: %s sentence-initial negations in one paragraph - collapse to a positive statement.

rules.name: signpost-opener
rules.message: Signpost opener '%s' - delete the announcement and state the thing.
patterns: (?:^|[.!?]\s+|["'(]\s*)(?:below is|below are|here is how|here'?s how|here is what|here'?s the thing|here'?s the deal|here'?s why|what follows is|in this section|in this (?:guide|article|post)|let'?s dive|let'?s explore|let'?s unpack|let'?s break|let'?s start by|let'?s (?:look|talk|walk) through|without further ado|we'?ll explore)\b

rules.name: em-dash
rules.message: Em/en dash or double-hyphen ('%s') - target zero; use a period, comma, or parentheses.

rules.name: tricolon
rules.message: Possible rule-of-three near '%s' - three parallel items joined by 'and'/'or'; vary the rhythm or cut one.

rules.name: aphorism
rules.message: Aphorism formula '%s' - replace with the concrete claim it gestures at.

rules.name: generic-temporal-opener
rules.message: Generic temporal opener '%s' - drop the throat-clearing and open on the substance.

rules.name: balancing-hedge
rules.message: Balancing hedge '%s' - the "While X, it also/still Y" frame is filler; lead with the point.

rules.name: staccato
rules.message: Staccato: %s short sentences in a row - clipped declaratives read as manufactured drama; connect some of them or vary the length.
